# Workspace Invariants: Native Config Injection

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/workspaceinvariantsdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to workspace invariants](../invariants.md)

C6 owns the injection mechanism. Policy bundles decide what must exist; Workspace creates files deterministically under provider-standard locations inside the task workdir.

- `CLAUDE.md`, loaded by Claude Code.
- `AGENTS.md`, loaded by Codex and default instruction-only providers.
- Claude `.claude/settings.json` and hook settings when C7 allows native hooks.
- `.riido/native-config-manifest.json` using `riido-native-config-manifest.v1`.
- `.riido/` run metadata such as task, policy bundle lock, and native config lock.

If SaaS task source provides `riido_telemetry_contract`, supervisor injects the hard rule independently of provider prompt placement. Telemetry contract changes participate in `NativeConfigVersion` input hashes.

SaaS agent presentation fields such as thumbnail URL and description belong to public contracts and control-plane; daemon does not inject those values into native config.
